180	- A metrics schema MUST have at least one entry.
   181	
   182	### 6.2 Reserved names
   183	
   184	The names `trial_id`, `commit_sha`, `parent_commits`, `branch`, `status`, `artifacts_uri`, `description`, `timestamp`, `started_at`, `completed_at` are reserved by the protocol for use on the trial object and MUST NOT appear in a metrics schema.
   185	
   186	## 7. Trial
   187	
   188	Schema: [`schemas/trial.schema.json`](schemas/trial.schema.json).
   189	
   190	A trial is one completed attempt.
   191	
   192	### 7.1 Fields
   193	
   194	| Field | Required | Type | Description |
   195	|---|---|---|---|
   196	| `trial_id` | yes | string | Unique identifier. |
   197	| `experiment_id` | yes | string | The experiment this trial belongs to. |
   198	| `proposal_id` | yes | string | The proposal that produced this trial. |
   199	| `status` | yes | string | One of `"starting"`, `"success"`, `"error"`, `"eval_error"`. |
   200	| `parent_commits` | yes | array of SHA | Inherited from the proposal. |
   201	| `branch` | no | string | Worker branch under `work/*`. Present once the implementer starts. |
   202	| `commit_sha` | no | string | Worker-branch tip SHA. Present once the implementer completes. |
   203	| `trial_commit_sha` | no | string | Canonical-lineage SHA under `trial/*`. Present once the integrator has promoted the trial. |
   204	| `artifacts_uri` | no | string (URI) | Where the trial's artifacts live. |
   205	| `description` | no | string | Human-readable summary. |
   206	| `metrics` | no | object | Metrics payload; shape dictated by the experiment's metrics schema. |
   207	| `started_at` | yes | timestamp | When the implementer began. |
   208	| `completed_at` | no | timestamp | Set when the trial reaches a terminal status. Written exactly once by the orchestrator, atomically with the transition from `"starting"` to `"success"`, `"error"`, or `"eval_error"` (see [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3 and [`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §4.4). |
   209	
   210	### 7.2 Metrics payload
   211	
   212	If present, `metrics` MUST be an object whose keys are a subset of the declared metrics-schema keys and whose values match the declared types (§1.3) or are `null`. Because the metrics-schema is per-experiment, [`schemas/trial.schema.json`](schemas/trial.schema.json) cannot express this constraint generically and leaves `metrics` as an open object; the per-metric type check is a runtime responsibility of the orchestrator. A conforming orchestrator MUST reject a metrics payload that violates the experiment's metrics-schema, and MUST NOT record the trial as `"success"` in that case.
   213	
   214	### 7.3 Status transitions
   215	
   216	Trial status transitions are behavioral and are defined alongside the task and event protocols in [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md). The data model constrains only that the status be one of the listed strings.
   217	
   218	## 8. Invariants across entities
   219	
   220	This section collects structural invariants that span multiple entities. Behavioral invariants (claim semantics, transactional event writes) live in the corresponding behavior chapters.
   221	
   222	1. **Identifier scope.** `task_id`, `proposal_id`, `trial_id`, and `event_id` MUST each be unique within a single experiment. Uniqueness across experiments is not required by the protocol but is RECOMMENDED when an implementation shares a store across experiments.
   223	2. **Reference integrity.** Every `proposal_id` referenced by a task payload or trial MUST name a proposal persisted in the store before the reference is written. Every `trial_id` referenced by an `evaluate` task MUST exist with `status == "starting"` at dispatch time.
   224	3. **Metrics/schema conformance.** Every metrics payload persisted on a trial MUST validate against the experiment's `metrics_schema` (§6).
   225	4. **Canonical-lineage single-writer.** Only the integrator MAY write to `trial_commit_sha` on a trial. Workers MUST NOT set or modify this field directly.
   226	5. **Claim uniqueness.** A task MAY have at most one active `claim` object at any time.

1	# Storage
     2	
     3	This chapter specifies the three durable stores an EDEN deployment depends on — the **task store**, the **event log**, and the **artifact store** — as protocol-level contracts. It pins the operations each store MUST expose, the consistency and durability properties those operations MUST honor, and the invariants spanning stores that a conforming deployment MUST preserve.
     4	
     5	The stores are introduced in [`01-concepts.md`](01-concepts.md) §10. The entities they hold are defined in [`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md); the behavioral contracts consuming them are in [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md), [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md), and [`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md). This chapter specifies the storage-side contract those behaviors rely on.
     6	
     7	A store is specified by **what observable guarantees it provides**, not by any particular mechanism. A conforming task store MAY be backed by a relational database, an embedded key-value engine, or an in-memory structure; the same is true of the event log and artifact store. Conformance is behavioral.
     8	
     9	## 1. Task store
    10	
    11	The task store holds the set of tasks and their current state for an experiment. It MUST support the operations below, each with the listed atomicity and failure semantics. Wire-level transport is a binding concern and is not pinned here.
    12	
    13	### 1.1 Operations
    14	
    15	| Operation | Arguments | Effect |
    16	|---|---|---|
    17	| `create_task` | fully-formed `task` object with `state == "pending"` | Atomically inserts the task and appends `task.created` to the event log ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)). |
    18	| `claim` | `task_id`, `worker_id`, OPTIONAL `expires_at` | Atomically transitions a `pending` task to `claimed`, issues a fresh claim token, and appends `task.claimed`. |
    19	| `submit` | `task_id`, `token`, role-specific result | Atomically transitions a `claimed` task to `submitted`, persists the result, and appends `task.submitted`. Idempotent per [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.2. |
    20	| `accept` | `task_id`, role-specific acceptance payload | Atomically transitions `submitted → completed`, applies the role-specific effect (e.g. proposal completion, trial status write), clears `claim`, and appends `task.completed` plus any composite events. |
    21	| `reject` | `task_id`, `reason`, role-specific failure payload | Atomically transitions `submitted → failed`, applies the role-specific effect, clears `claim`, and appends `task.failed` plus any composite events. |
    22	| `reclaim` | `task_id`, OPTIONAL `cause` | Atomically transitions `claimed`/`submitted` back to `pending` (where permitted; [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §5.1), invalidates the token, clears `claim`, performs §2.2 composite effects where applicable, and appends `task.reclaimed`. |
    23	| `read_task` | `task_id` | Returns the current task object or a well-defined not-found signal. |
    24	| `list_tasks` | filter (`kind`, `state`, `experiment_id`, …) | Returns matching tasks. Ordering is implementation-defined. |
    25	
    26	"Atomically" means: the state change, the result-payload write, any cross-entity effect listed in [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §2.2, and the event appends are all durable together or none of them is ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)).
    27	
    28	### 1.2 Atomic claim (serialization)
    29	
    30	For any given `pending` task, at most one concurrent `claim` invocation MUST succeed. A conforming task store MUST expose this as a linearizable operation: after a successful claim, every subsequent reader of the task observes `state == "claimed"` with the new claim object; no reader observes an intermediate state without the claim.
    31	
    32	The serialization mechanism (row lock + update, compare-and-set on version, `SELECT … FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`) is implementation-defined. The observable guarantee is not.
    33	
    34	### 1.3 Token-authorization rule
    35	
    36	`submit` and any other claim-holder operation MUST reject calls whose presented token does not equal the token currently recorded on the task's `claim` object ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §3.3). A conforming task store MUST NOT use `worker_id` as a substitute for the token when authorizing.
    37	
    38	### 1.4 Idempotent resubmit
    39	
    40	The idempotency rule for `submit` is specified normatively in [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.2. A conforming task store MUST implement it: a resubmit carrying the current token, with a content-equivalent payload, MUST succeed without mutating state; an inconsistent resubmit MUST be rejected.
    41	
    42	### 1.5 Terminal immutability
    43	
    44	Once a task is `completed` or `failed` ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.4), no subsequent operation MAY change any task field except audit-only metadata the task store documents outside the normative schema. In particular, `reclaim` MUST be rejected on terminal tasks.
    45	
    46	### 1.6 Per-task serialization
    47	
    48	All mutating operations on a single task MUST be serialized: the observable history of a task is a total order ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §6.1). A conforming task store MUST NOT expose a state that has not yet been accompanied by its event ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)).
    49	
    50	### 1.7 Proposal and trial persistence
    51	
    52	Proposals ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §5) and trials (§7) are persisted alongside tasks. A conforming deployment MAY back them with the task store itself, with a separate store, or with any combination; the protocol constrains the observable contract, not the physical layout. Every conforming deployment MUST expose, for both proposals and trials, at minimum:
    53	
    54	- **Create** — durably insert a new object whose fields validate against the corresponding schema ([`schemas/proposal.schema.json`](schemas/proposal.schema.json), [`schemas/trial.schema.json`](schemas/trial.schema.json)).
    55	- **Read** — return the current object or a well-defined not-found signal.
    56	- **List** — return objects matching a filter (by `experiment_id`, `state`/`status`, etc.). Ordering is implementation-defined.
    57	- **Update** — apply the field writes the role contracts and task- terminal transitions specify ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §2.2, §3.2, §4.4; [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3, §5.4).
    58	
    59	All proposal and trial writes MUST commit atomically with the accompanying event(s) per §6. The durability (§3.1), read-after-write (§3.2), crash-recovery (§3.3), and no-fabrication (§3.4) rules apply uniformly. Terminal immutability applies to proposals in `completed` and trials in `success`/`error`/`eval_error` — the `trial_commit_sha` field is the **one** post-terminal write permitted on a trial, written exclusively by the integrator ([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §3.4), and it MUST be written atomically with its event and its `trial/*` ref.
    60	
    61	## 2. Event log
    62	
    63	The event log holds the per-experiment event stream. It MUST support the operations below and MUST honor the delivery guarantees specified in [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.
    64	
    65	### 2.1 Operations
    66	
    67	| Operation | Arguments | Effect |
    68	|---|---|---|
    69	| `append` | fully-formed event object | Atomically appends the event, together with the state change it describes ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)). |
    70	| `read_range` | experiment_id, OPTIONAL cursor | Returns a durable, ordered range of events. |
    71	| `subscribe` | experiment_id, OPTIONAL starting cursor | Returns a stream that delivers events in log order until cancelled. |
    72	| `replay` | experiment_id | Returns the full stream from the first event ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.4). |
    73	
    74	`append` MUST NOT be exposed as a standalone operation that the task store, proposal store, or trial store can call independently of the state change the event describes. The atomicity contract ([§6](#6-transactional-guarantees)) requires the append to travel with the state change; the event log operation is a primitive of the composite transaction, not an independent one.
    75	
    76	### 2.2 Ordering and causality
    77	
    78	The log MUST present a single total order per experiment ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.1) that respects causality ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.2). The ordering mechanism is implementation-defined; the observable guarantee is not.
    79	
    80	The log MAY provide a global ordering across experiments as an optimization, but MUST NOT require subscribers to depend on it ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.6).
    81	
    82	### 2.3 Delivery
    83	
    84	At-least-once delivery is the minimum contract ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.3). Implementations MAY offer exactly-once via durable offset commits.
    85	
    86	### 2.4 Retention
    87	
    88	For the lifetime of an experiment — from registration until the experiment reaches its operator-declared terminal state — a conforming event log MUST retain **every** appended event for that experiment and MUST serve full replay from the first event ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §4.4). No compaction, archival, or deletion MAY occur inside this window. A deployment MAY compact or archive an experiment's events once it has reached its operator-declared terminal state; after compaction the log MUST either (a) reject replays older than the compaction horizon with a well-defined error, or (b) serve those replays from an archive that preserves order and content. It MUST NOT silently drop events from a replay.
    89	
    90	## 3. Durability
    91	
    92	The following applies uniformly to the task store and the event log.
    93	
    94	### 3.1 Write durability
    95	
    96	An operation that the store acknowledges to its caller MUST survive a subsequent crash of the store's host. "Acknowledgement" is the return value indicating success; a store MUST NOT return success for a write that has not yet been persisted to durable media (or the equivalent quorum in a replicated implementation).
    97	
    98	### 3.2 Read-after-write consistency
    99	
   100	After a successful write, every subsequent read from any reader MUST observe the effect of the write. A conforming store MUST NOT expose a stale view of data after acknowledging the write that would update it. (Subscribers receive events via the log's delivery mechanism, not by polling the task store, so the read-after-write rule applies to direct reads; event delivery is governed by §4 of the event protocol.)
   101	
   102	### 3.3 Crash recovery
   103	
   104	A store that crashes mid-operation MUST recover to a state in which every acknowledged write is visible and no partially-applied operation is visible. Implementations that use multi-step transactions MUST ensure rollback on failure so that partial state never becomes observable.
   105	
   106	### 3.4 No fabrication
   107	
   108	A store MUST NOT materialize an event, a task, a proposal, or a trial from nothing. Every persisted object MUST originate from a caller- driven operation. In particular, a conforming event log MUST NOT synthesize events the caller did not `append`.
   109	
   110	## 4. Per-experiment metrics schemas
   111	
   112	Every experiment declares a metrics schema in its `experiment_config` ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §1.3, [`schemas/metrics-schema.schema.json`](schemas/metrics-schema.schema.json)). The task store (or an equivalently-scoped component in a conforming deployment) MUST enforce the following at every write that touches trial metrics.
   113	
   114	### 4.1 Registration
   115	
   116	At experiment registration time, a conforming deployment MUST persist the experiment's metrics schema durably and atomically with the experiment's other configuration. A subsequent write of a trial's `metrics` field MUST be validated against the schema registered for that experiment; no write MAY bypass this validation.
   117	
   118	### 4.2 Immutability during an experiment
   119	
   120	A metrics schema MUST NOT be mutated for the lifetime of the experiment. An operator who wishes to change the metrics set MUST register a new experiment (Phase 12 defines the control-plane operations that govern this, but the invariant applies from v0 onward). A conforming deployment MUST reject any in-flight mutation of an existing experiment's metrics schema.
   121	
   122	The rationale is canonicality: comparing trials across an experiment only has meaning if the metric definitions they are compared on did not move during the experiment.
   123	
   124	### 4.3 Per-metric type checks
   125	
   126	A successful write of a `trial.metrics` payload MUST satisfy:
   127	
   128	- Every key in `metrics` is present in the experiment's metrics schema.
   129	- Every value either satisfies the declared type of its key — per the type mapping in [`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §1.3 (`integer`, `real`, `text`) — or is `null`.
   130	- No reserved name ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §6.2) appears as a key.
   131	
   132	A write that violates any of these MUST be rejected; the store MUST NOT record a trial as `success` with an invalid metrics payload ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.2).
   133	
   134	## 5. Artifact store
   135	
   136	The artifact store holds files the roles produce (plan text, code diffs, evaluator outputs, logs) and exposes them by URI. It is less load-bearing than the task store and event log — the protocol uses it only for content referenced by `artifacts_uri` on proposals and trials — and its contract is accordingly narrower.
   137	
   138	### 5.1 Operations
   139	
   140	A conforming artifact store MUST support:
   141	
   142	- **Upload** — given content bytes and a proposed identity (naming scheme is implementation-defined), persist the bytes and return a URI that will later resolve to them.
   143	- **Fetch** — given a URI the store issued, return the byte content the upload persisted.
   144	
   145	URIs MUST be RFC 3986–conformant ([`schemas/proposal.schema.json`](schemas/proposal.schema.json) enforces this on the proposal side).
   146	
   147	### 5.2 Durability
   148	
   149	Once the artifact store returns a URI to an uploader, the content at that URI MUST remain resolvable until the experiment's retention window elapses. A conforming deployment MUST define its retention window explicitly. A URI recorded on a `trial/*` commit's eval manifest — whether as the optional `artifacts_uri` field ([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §4.2) or inside the optional per-file `artifacts` inventory ([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §4.4) — MUST be resolvable for at least as long as that `trial/*` commit is retained.
   150	
   151	### 5.3 Content integrity
   152	
   153	The artifact store MUST NOT serve byte content that differs from what was originally uploaded for a given URI. A store that versions URIs MUST ensure a fetch against a given URI returns the upload that produced it, not a subsequent overwrite.
   154	
   155	When the optional per-file `artifacts` inventory ([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §4.4) is present on a `trial/*` commit, the manifest's recorded `sha256` and `bytes` fields are the canonical attestation of that file's byte content at integration time; the artifact store is not required to compute or verify them at fetch time, but its no-overwrite rule is what makes the attestation load-bearing.
   156	
   157	### 5.4 No protocol-owned mutation after the fact
   158	
   159	Once a protocol-owned object (proposal, trial, eval manifest) references an artifact URI, a conforming deployment MUST NOT overwrite the content at that URI. Overwriting would break reproducibility guarantees that subscribers reading the event log rely on.
   160	
   161	## 6. Transactional guarantees
   162	
   163	The transactional invariant established in [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §2 is the central storage contract:
   164	
   165	> Every state change observable via tasks, proposals, or trials MUST > be accompanied by a corresponding event, and the event write MUST > be atomic with the state change it describes.
   166	
   167	A conforming deployment MUST satisfy this invariant for every write that mutates protocol-owned state — the task operations in §1.1, the proposal and trial operations in §1.7, and every composite commit in [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §2.2. The mechanism is implementation-defined. The observable guarantee — that no reader observes a state change without its event and vice versa — is not.
   168	
   169	### 6.1 Composite commits
   170	
   171	For the multi-entity transitions enumerated in [`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §2.2, every component of the commit (multiple state changes + multiple event appends) MUST land together or not at all. A subscriber that observes any one event of a composite commit MUST be able to observe all of them.
   172	
   173	### 6.2 Failure semantics
   174	
   175	If any component of a composite commit fails, the deployment MUST roll back all other components. Operator-facing error reporting is implementation-defined; the observable requirement is that no partial commit reaches any reader.
   176	
   177	### 6.3 Recovery after partial commit
   178	
   179	If a crash occurs mid-commit, recovery (§3.3) MUST complete or roll back the transaction such that the composite-commit invariant (§6.1) holds for every reader after recovery. A conforming deployment MAY use journal replay, two-phase commit resolution, or equivalent mechanisms.
   180	
   181	## 7. Implementation latitude
   182	
   183	The protocol leaves to implementations:
   184	
   185	- The storage technology for each store (RDBMS, KV store, embedded engine, distributed log, …).
   186	- The wire transport for store access (local library call, HTTP, gRPC, direct SQL).
   187	- Whether the three stores are backed by three separate systems, one system serving all three, or any combination (e.g. a single RDBMS whose outbox table is the event log).
   188	- The retention policies for events, work branches, and artifacts, provided the minimum retention floors in §2.4, §5.2, and [`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md) §1.3 are met.
   189	- The concrete representation of claim tokens, event IDs, and URIs.
   190	
   191	What the protocol does **not** leave to implementations:
   192	
   193	- The set of operations each store MUST expose (§1.1, §2.1, §5.1).
   194	- The atomicity contract of each operation (§1, §6).
   195	- The read-after-write and crash-recovery guarantees (§3).
   196	- The metrics-schema enforcement rules (§4).
   197	- The content-immutability rule on artifacts referenced by protocol-owned objects (§5.4).

   867

   869	        artifacts_uri (``03-roles.md`` §4.4).

   885	    def integrate_trial(self, trial_id: str, trial_commit_sha: str) -> None:
   886	        """Integrator promotion: write ``trial_commit_sha`` and emit ``trial.integrated``.
   887	
   888	        Per ``08-storage.md`` §1.7: ``trial_commit_sha`` is the one
   889	        post-terminal write permitted on a trial; it must be written
   890	        atomically with its event.
   891	
   892	        **Same-value idempotency** (``07-wire-protocol.md`` §5): a
   893	        repeated call whose ``trial_commit_sha`` equals the value
   894	        already stored on the trial is a no-op and MUST NOT append a
   895	        second ``trial.integrated`` event. This rule lets an HTTP-
   896	        mediated caller retry a transport-indeterminate
   897	        ``integrate_trial`` request without risking double-commit;
   898	        the same-value branch also keeps direct-``Store`` callers
   899	        and wire-mediated callers on identical contracts.
   900	
   901	        A repeated call with a **different** ``trial_commit_sha``
   902	        raises ``InvalidPrecondition`` — the chapter 6 §1.2 sole-
   903	        writer rule has been violated and operator intervention is
   904	        required. The caller (e.g. ``Integrator``) maps this to an
   905	        ``AtomicityViolation`` rather than compensating the ref.
   906	        """
   907	        with self._atomic_operation():
   908	            trial = self._require_trial(trial_id)
   909	            if trial.status != "success":
   910	                raise InvalidPrecondition(
   911	                    f"trial {trial_id!r} must be in 'success' to integrate, "
   912	                    f"not {trial.status!r}"
   913	                )
   914	            if trial.trial_commit_sha is not None:
   915	                if trial.trial_commit_sha == trial_commit_sha:
   916	                    return
   917	                raise InvalidPrecondition(
   918	                    f"trial {trial_id!r} is already integrated with a "
   919	                    f"different trial_commit_sha "
   920	                    f"({trial.trial_commit_sha!r} != {trial_commit_sha!r})"
   921	                )
   922	            tx = _Tx()
   923	            tx.trials[trial_id] = _validated_update(
   924	                trial, trial_commit_sha=trial_commit_sha
   925	            )
   926	            tx.events.append(
   927	                self._event(
   928	                    "trial.integrated",
   929	                    {"trial_id": trial_id, "trial_commit_sha": trial_commit_sha},
   930	                )
   931	            )
   932	            self._apply_commit(tx)

1	"""Trial — one in-flight or completed attempt to improve the objective.
     2	
     3	Mirrors ``spec/v0/schemas/trial.schema.json``. The ``metrics`` object's shape
     4	is constrained by the experiment's ``metrics_schema``; this model does not
     5	reproduce that constraint because metrics keys are per-experiment.
     6	"""
     7	
     8	from __future__ import annotations
     9	
    10	from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal
    11	
    12	from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, StringConstraints
    13	
    14	from ._common import CommitSha, DateTimeStr, NotNone, UriStr
    15	
    16	TrialStatus = Literal["starting", "success", "error", "eval_error"]
    17	"""Trial lifecycle statuses; ``success``, ``error``, ``eval_error`` are terminal."""
    18

    20	WorkBranch = Annotated[str, StringConstraints(pattern=WORK_BRANCH_PATTERN)]

    31	    status: TrialStatus

    34	    commit_sha: Annotated[CommitSha | None, NotNone] = None
    35	    trial_commit_sha: Annotated[CommitSha | None, NotNone] = None
    36	    artifacts_uri: Annotated[UriStr | None, NotNone] = None
    37	    description: Annotated[str | None, NotNone] = None
    38	    metrics: Annotated[dict[str, Any] | None, NotNone] = None
    39	    started_at: DateTimeStr
    40	    completed_at: Annotated[DateTimeStr | None, NotNone] = None

item.text: There’s one deeper question behind the scope decision: whether some “git-aware” chapter-06 MUSTs are still black-box testable by driving impossible trial state through the wire and observing integrate reject. I’m checking the current reference adapter and server shape to see whether the plan is conflating “no repo access from the harness” with “no repo-sensitive behavior testable at all.”

1	"""Reference IUT adapter — spawns the EDEN reference task-store-server.
     2	
     3	The adapter is per-scenario: each test gets a fresh subprocess. Bonds
     4	to in-memory SQLite storage for speed; conformance does not test
     5	durability (eden-storage's own tests do).
     6	"""
     7	
     8	from __future__ import annotations
     9	
    10	import queue
    11	import secrets
    12	import subprocess
    13	import sys
    14	import threading
    15	import time
    16	from pathlib import Path
    17	
    18	from conformance.harness.adapter import IutAdapter, IutHandle
    19	
    20	
    21	class ReferenceAdapter(IutAdapter):
    22	    """Spawns ``python -m eden_task_store_server`` in a subprocess."""
    23	
    24	    _PORT_ANNOUNCE_TIMEOUT = 15.0
    25	    _STOP_TIMEOUT = 5.0
    26	    _SUBSCRIBE_TIMEOUT_S = 2.0  # see plan §D rationale
    27	
    28	    def __init__(self) -> None:
    29	        self._proc: subprocess.Popen[str] | None = None
    30	        self._stderr_lines: list[str] = []
    31	        self._stderr_thread: threading.Thread | None = None
    32	
    33	    def start(
    34	        self,
    35	        *,
    36	        experiment_config_path: Path,
    37	        experiment_id: str,
    38	    ) -> IutHandle:
    39	        # Use token_hex (only [0-9a-f]) — token_urlsafe can start with
    40	        # `-`, which argparse interprets as a new flag and the server
    41	        # exits with code 2 before announcing its port.
    42	        token = secrets.token_hex(24)
    43	        self._proc = subprocess.Popen(
    44	            [
    45	                sys.executable,
    46	                "-m",
    47	                "eden_task_store_server",
    48	                "--store-url",
    49	                ":memory:",
    50	                "--experiment-id",
    51	                experiment_id,
    52	                "--experiment-config",
    53	                str(experiment_config_path),
    54	                "--shared-token",
    55	                token,
    56	                "--subscribe-timeout",
    57	                str(self._SUBSCRIBE_TIMEOUT_S),
    58	                "--port",
    59	                "0",
    60	                "--host",
    61	                "127.0.0.1",
    62	            ],
    63	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
    64	            stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
    65	            text=True,
    66	        )
    67	        # Defensive: if the port-announce read or stderr-drain start
    68	        # raises, the spawned subprocess and its drain thread would leak
    69	        # because the fixture never reached `yield` — `stop()` would not
    70	        # be called. Catch, clean up, then re-raise.
    71	        try:
    72	            port = self._read_port_announcement()
    73	            self._start_stderr_drain()
    74	        except BaseException:
    75	            self.stop()
    76	            raise
    77	        return IutHandle(
    78	            base_url=f"http://127.0.0.1:{port}",
    79	            experiment_id=experiment_id,
    80	            extra_headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
    81	        )
    82	
    83	    def stop(self) -> None:
    84	        proc = self._proc
    85	        if proc is None:
    86	            return
    87	        if proc.poll() is None:
    88	            proc.terminate()
    89	            try:
    90	                proc.wait(timeout=self._STOP_TIMEOUT)
    91	            except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
    92	                proc.kill()
    93	                proc.wait(timeout=2.0)
    94	        if self._stderr_thread is not None:
    95	            self._stderr_thread.join(timeout=1.0)
    96	        self._proc = None
    97	
    98	    @property
    99	    def stderr_log(self) -> str:
   100	        return "".join(self._stderr_lines)
   101	
   102	    # ----- internals -----
   103	
   104	    def _read_port_announcement(self) -> int:
   105	        proc = self._proc
   106	        assert proc is not None and proc.stdout is not None
   107	        # readline() blocks indefinitely until a newline OR EOF, so a
   108	        # hung server that never prints anything would wedge here for
   109	        # the lifetime of the test process. Drain stdout from a
   110	        # daemon thread into a queue so the timeout is real.
   111	        q: queue.Queue[str] = queue.Queue()
   112	
   113	        def _reader() -> None:
   114	            assert proc.stdout is not None
   115	            try:
   116	                for line in iter(proc.stdout.readline, ""):
   117	                    q.put(line)
   118	            finally:
   119	                q.put("")  # EOF sentinel
   120	
   121	        reader = threading.Thread(target=_reader, daemon=True)
   122	        reader.start()
   123	        deadline = time.monotonic() + self._PORT_ANNOUNCE_TIMEOUT
   124	        while True:
   125	            remaining = deadline - time.monotonic()
   126	            if remaining <= 0:
   127	                raise RuntimeError(
   128	                    "task-store-server did not announce port within "
   129	                    f"{self._PORT_ANNOUNCE_TIMEOUT}s; stderr="
   130	                    f"{self.stderr_log!r}"
   131	                )
   132	            try:
   133	                line = q.get(timeout=min(remaining, 0.5))
   134	            except queue.Empty:
   135	                # Reader still alive, no line yet — loop and re-check.
   136	                if proc.poll() is not None:
   137	                    raise RuntimeError(
   138	                        f"task-store-server exited early with code "
   139	                        f"{proc.returncode}; stderr="
   140	                        f"{self._read_remaining_stderr()!r}"
   141	                    ) from None
   142	                continue
   143	            if line == "":
   144	                # EOF: the subprocess closed stdout, so it likely exited.
   145	                raise RuntimeError(
   146	                    f"task-store-server stdout closed before announcing "
   147	                    f"port; rc={proc.poll()!r}; stderr="
   148	                    f"{self._read_remaining_stderr()!r}"
   149	                )
   150	            if line.startswith("EDEN_TASK_STORE_LISTENING"):
   151	                parts = dict(p.split("=", 1) for p in line.strip().split()[1:])
   152	                return int(parts["port"])
   153

item.text:
**Findings**

1. High: the scope deferral is too broad. The plan treats the git-aware parts of chapter 06 as untestable through a wire-only suite ([plan](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-11d-integrator-conformance.md:40>), [plan](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-11d-integrator-conformance.md:156>)), but §2’s “`commit_sha` is set and resolves to a commit on the trial’s `branch`” has a wire-observable consequence: `/integrate` must reject and must not write `trial_commit_sha` or emit `trial.integrated` ([06-integrator.md](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/06-integrator.md:41>)). The harness can seed that state through normal endpoints by creating a `starting` trial with arbitrary `branch`/`commit_sha`, driving only the evaluator leg to `success`, then calling `/integrate`. Deferring that case leaves a real chapter-06 MUST untested.

2. High: chapter 06 §5.3 is not actually covered by the proposed group/citation shape. The plan says existing v1 `Integrate idempotency` coverage is enough ([plan](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-11d-integrator-conformance.md:11>), [plan](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-11d-integrator-conformance.md:51>)), but those tests cite chapter 07 §5, not chapter 06 §5.3 ([test_integrate_idempotency.py](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/conformance/scenarios/test_integrate_idempotency.py:15>)). If chunk 11d is the chapter-06 addendum, at least one new scenario should cite §5.3, or the chapter-9 index row for `Integrator atomicity` should include §5.3. As written, `divergent-resubmit-leaves-no-second-event` is really a repeat-promotion / no-overwrite assertion, not purely §3.4 atomic-write ([06-integrator.md](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/06-integrator.md:185>)).

3. Medium: one proposed atomicity test is weaker than the missing coverage it displaces. `event-implies-field` is close to a restatement of `field-implies-event`, and `failed-integrate-leaves-no-artifacts` is really a §2 precondition rejection case, not the strongest §3.4-specific delta ([plan](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-11d-integrator-conformance.md:102>), [plan](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-11d-integrator-conformance.md:114>)). I would spend one of those slots on the missing “success trial with bogus branch/commit rejects integrate” scenario instead.

4. Medium: the plan underestimates the reference-IUT impact. The current conformance adapter only spawns `eden_task_store_server` ([adapter.py](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/conformance/src/conformance/adapters/reference/adapter.py:1>)), and the reference `integrate_trial` implementation behind that server checks status/idempotency only; it does not perform repo-backed chapter-06 promotion validation ([eden_storage/_base.py](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/_base.py:885>)). If 11d adds the missing §2 branch/commit test, the plan needs to acknowledge likely adapter/IUT work instead of assuming “no adapter changes.”

**Overall assessment**

Needs revisions.

**By question**

1. Spec scope correctness: partly right. Deferring direct git-artifact assertions for squash shape, manifest shape, `work/*`, and ref durability is the right shape. Blanket deferral of repo-sensitive promotion preconditions is too aggressive; at least the “`commit_sha` resolves on `branch`” refusal path is black-box testable.

2. Coverage completeness: the plan misses one wire-observable §2 MUST: reject integrate for a `success` trial whose recorded `branch`/`commit_sha` is not a real branch-tip commit. It also treats chapter 06 §5.3 as already covered, but existing coverage is only via chapter 07 citations and does not satisfy chapter-06 traceability.

3. Test design: the cross-artifact consistency framing is good, and reading state after the call returns matches §3.4’s “once a promotion has returned” wording ([06-integrator.md](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/06-integrator.md:100>)). I would keep one success-path consistency test, one event-side/cardinality retry test, and shift one of the current duplicates to the missing §2 invalid-branch/commit case.

4. Citation-check discipline: `check_citations.py` will accept a separate v1+roles+integrator table inside chapter 9 §5; it parses all rows until the next `##` ([check_citations.py](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/conformance/src/conformance/tools/check_citations.py:49>)). But if you want a test to cite chapter 06 §5.3, the matching chapter-9 row must also call out §5.3, or the group-relevance check will fail ([check_citations.py](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/conformance/src/conformance/tools/check_citations.py:150>), [check_citations.py](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/conformance/src/conformance/tools/check_citations.py:295>)).

5. Helper extensions: `drive_to_error_trial` and `drive_to_eval_error_trial` are good additions and match the hardened `_seed` pattern. They are not enough for the missing invalid-branch/commit case; that needs either a new helper for “manual starting trial -> evaluator success” or an extension to `drive_to_success_trial`.

6. Inter-chapter restatement drift: the proposed chapter-9 amendment is directionally correct, but I would narrow it. Better resolution: chapter 9 should say the wire-only suite covers wire-observable consequences of chapter 06 §2 / §3.4 / §5.3, while direct inspection of git artifacts remains deferred to a future binding.

7. Other risks/gaps: chapter 9 §5 prose currently says the integrator level adds “its own group” singular ([09-conformance.md](</Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/09-conformance.md:61>)); if 11d adds two groups, that text should be updated too. The metrics-schema-at-promotion case is reasonable to defer for now; I do not see a clean normative-endpoint path to seed a `success` trial with invalid metrics.
